utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright so how're you doing today angela

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing pretty well

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright so looking here at your appointment notes it says you're you're here you think you have a kidney stone you had some in the past so we're gon na take a look at that and then you also have a past medical history of hypertension and diabetes we want to take a look at those as well so first thing what's going on with your kidneys you as what how long ago have you been feeling pain and and how's all that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: pain's been up and down i went to the emergency room last week but now i think i'm doing a little bit better

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so the case of kidney stones so have you had you said you've had them in the past and how often would you say

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't know i this is probably like my seventh or eighth kidney stone

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: seven or eight kidney stones so do you think you passed it yet or is it still in the

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i think this one passed yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: this one passed how long would you say it took to

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well i went last week and then i think it passed about three days ago

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so are you still noticing any blood in your urine

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no no more blood

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright are you still having pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nope

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: the pain's gone

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay that that's that's that's really good did they do anything for you at the hospital giving you any medications

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: they gave me some pain medicine

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay do you remember what it was

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i think it was percocet

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay well that's good i'm very glad to see that you were able to pass that stone have you seen a urologist before about this

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have n't seen one in a while but yes i saw someone maybe a year ago

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so yeah i know you keep having these recurrent kidney stones so i definitely think we can get you a referral to urology just to check up on that and also wan na do some labs as well

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so i also see you're here for you have a past medical history of of hypertension and when you came in today your blood pressure was a little bit high it was a one fifty over ninety i'm reading here in your chart you're on two point five of norvasc

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hmmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: now have you been taking that regularly

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have but at home my blood pressure is always great

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay maybe you have a little white coat syndrome some of my patients do have it i have it myself and i'm a provider so i definitely understand yeah i know we we talked about last time you getting a blood pressure cuff and taking those about two to three times a week so what have those readings been i'm usually like one thirty to one forty over sixty to seventy

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's that's that's not too bad i think when you first came in you were around like one eighty so it seems to be that that norvasc is is working for you how about your diet i know you were having a little issue eating some fast food and and cakes and cookies and have you been able to get that under control

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah it's hard to give up the fast food altogether because it's a lot of on the go you know

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so do you think you would be able to get that under control by yourself or would you do you think you would need help with that maybe a dietitian be able to help you out

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i do n't know i do n't know if i can make another appointment i just add to the extra

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: less time to make food so

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah yeah definitely understand

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright yeah so i mean that's one thing we just got ta work one is your diet we try to keep you at least just twenty three hundred milligrams or less of sodium per day i know that's hard for a lot of salads and stuff i know it's hard for lot of people especially with all like the the prepackage foods we have around today so that's definitely something we we should work on

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so let's also look here you have a history of diabetes and so you're on that five hundred milligrams of metformin daily now have you been taking that as well

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i take my metformin

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so what have your blood sugars been running daily

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well i do n't check it very often

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but i think they've been pretty good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so i'm looking here i think last after your last visit you got a1c now was six . seven so it's a little bit high it's gone down a little bit since you were first diagnosed with the type two diabetes a year ago so i'm glad we're making progress with that as well alright so i'm just gon na do a quick physical exam on you before i do just wan na make sure are you having any chest pain today

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright any any belly pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright so i'm gon na listen to your lungs your lungs are clear bilaterally i do n't hear any crackles listen to your heart so on your heart exam i do hear that grade two out of six systolic ejection murmur and we already knew about that previously so it has n't gotten any worse so that's good so i'm gon na just press here in your abdomen because that you did have those kidney stones does that hurt

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright i'm gon na press here on your back

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no pain

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so on your abdomen exam of your abdomen i'm showing no tenderness to palpation of the abdomen or tenderness of the the cva either on the right side so that that's good i think that's pretty much cleared up so let's we'll talk a little bit about my assessment and plan for you and so my assessment you you did have the those kidney stones but i i think they are passed this time but i do want to get a couple of labs so we'll get a urinalysis

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright we'll get a urine culture just to make sure everything is is cleared up i also want to give you a referral to referral referral to urology

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: because you do keep having these all the time and so maybe there's something else going wrong and so they can help get that under control

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: can i see doctor harris

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: of course yeah we can we can get you that road to doctor harris and

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: he's not like

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: he's great he's he's he he he's great i've heard he does really good work so that'll be good so for the hypertension you seem to be doing well on the two . five of norvasc so we are not gon na make any changes to that do you need any refills right now

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no usually the pharmacy just sends them through when i call

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay great so we we wo n't we gave you refills with that i do wan na give you a consult to nutrition

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: just to help you with that diet

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: because i think that's a major factor of us eventually getting you off of all medications

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hmmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then for your diabetes i'm just keep you on that on that five hundred of metformin okay i think you're doing well with that as well also but i do want you to start taking your blood sugars if you can take them before every meal

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: just to gauge where you are so you can tell how much food you should actually be be eating

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i can try that

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so how does that that sound

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that sounds like a plan when should i come back and see you

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so you can you can come back in three months and we'll check up again i forgot you did tell me last time that you were having some issues with insomnia

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hmmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how is that going for you is it still happening

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i mean sometimes i stay awake just kinda worrying about things but but i've tried some meditation apps and that helps

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and i know we talked a little bit before about practicing proper sleep hygiene you know just making sure that all of your electronics are off you know dark room

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: cool room have you been doing that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well i do like to sleep with the tv on and my phone is right by my bed because i never know if someone's gon na call me you know

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah i i know i'm like apple i do n't know if you have an iphone or not but i know apple has this the the sleep mode now do that disturbance so you put that on

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's a good idea

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: interrupt you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay have you tried taking melatonin to sleep

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i used it a couple times but but it did n't seem to help that much

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay how about i do n't think i've prescribed you anything yet do you think you would need anything

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hmmm i do n't really wan na take any sleeping pills

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay that's understandable alright so for the last issue for the insomnia i'm just gon na have you take ten milligrams of melatonin as needed

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and just try i guess the best as possible to practice the proper sleep hygiene so you can get to sleep at night and and feel pretty rested

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so do you have anything any other questions for me

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no that's all

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright so we will see you in three months

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay sounds good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: like

detail_list.summary: The patient is here for a kidney stone, with a history of hypertension and diabetes.
Kidney stone
The doctor asks about the duration and nature of the patient's kidney stone pain.
The patient reports intermittent pain and an emergency room visit last week, but feels better now.
The patient reports this is her seventh or eighth kidney stone.
The patient believes the kidney stone has passed.
The patient believes the stone passed three days ago.
The doctor asks if the patient has hematuria.
The patient denies hematuria.
The doctor asks if the patient is still experiencing pain.
The patient denies abdominal pain.
The doctor asks if the patient received any medication at the hospital.
The patient reports receiving Percocet at the hospital.
The doctor asks if the patient has seen a urologist previously.
The patient saw a urologist about a year ago.
The doctor plans to refer the patient to urology and order labs.
Hypertension
The patient's blood pressure is 150/90 mm Hg.
Norvasc 2.5 mg
The doctor asks if the patient is taking Norvasc regularly.
The patient reports taking Norvasc regularly and states her blood pressure is good at home.
The patient reports home blood pressure readings of 130-140/60-70 mm Hg.
The doctor asks about the patient's diet and control of fast food intake.
The doctor asks if the patient needs help with diet management, such as a dietitian.
The doctor advises limiting sodium intake to 2300 mg or less per day.
Diabetes
Metformin 500 mg daily
The doctor asks if the patient is taking metformin regularly.
The patient confirms taking metformin.
The doctor asks about the patient's daily blood sugar readings.
The patient reports not checking blood sugar very often.
The patient's A1c is 6.7.
The doctor asks if the patient has chest pain.
The patient denies chest pain.
The doctor asks if the patient has abdominal pain.
The patient denies abdominal pain.
The doctor performs a physical exam: lungs are clear, heart has a grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur.
The abdominal exam shows no tenderness to palpation or CVA tenderness.
The doctor orders a urinalysis and urine culture.
The doctor provides a referral to urology.
The patient asks to see Dr. Harris.
The doctor confirms the referral to Dr. Harris.
Continue Norvasc 2.5 mg.
The doctor asks if the patient needs medication refills.
The doctor provides a nutrition consult.
Continue metformin 500 mg daily.
The doctor instructs the patient to check blood sugar before every meal.
The patient will return for follow-up in 3 months.
Insomnia
The doctor asks about the status of the patient's insomnia.
The patient reports insomnia due to worry and uses meditation apps.
The doctor asks if the patient has tried melatonin.
The patient reports using melatonin previously without much success.
The doctor recommends taking 10 mg of melatonin as needed.
